_cite.reason: URL-normalization 1c: strip trailing markdown-emphasis `*`/backtick glued to citation URLs in intelligence_item_sections.content_md (customer-checkable link + validate_item_provenance criterion-2 exact-compare). Operator ruling 2026-07-04. Same transform as the write-site fix.

prior.content_md:
**A. Electronic System Connectivity (TRACE-DM Platform)**
Authorized waste collection operators must build or configure a direct API or portal connection to the AFM-managed TRACE-DM centralized platform. The system must be capable of registering each citizen-waste-handover transaction within a 24-hour window. For operators already using proprietary waste management software, the compliance cost is assessed as limited in the draft HG's explanatory memorandum, since most have existing record-keeping systems. However, formal TRACE-DM connectivity (not merely internal record-keeping) is a new technical requirement. Gap: no current centralized electronic registration obligation exists for citizen-to-operator waste flows; TRACE-DM introduces this de novo.

**B. Identity Verification Infrastructure**
Collection points must implement a documented citizen identity-check process at intake: verification of national ID or equivalent document, recording of CNP (personal identification code), and collection of a signed household-origin declaration. This requires staff training, updated intake SOPs, and printed or digital declaration form templates. Scope: all collection points receiving recyclable waste from private individuals in Romania. Deadline: entry into force of the final HG (date TBC post-consultation).

**C. Document Retention Systems**
All transaction records, declarations, and supporting documents must be retained for a minimum of **3 years**. Operators must ensure their document management systems (digital or physical) meet this retention standard and can produce records on demand during National Environmental Guard inspections.

**D. Metallic Waste Receipt Generation**
For ferrous and non-ferrous metal waste, the TRACE-DM system will generate the acquisition receipt (*borderou de achiziție*) electronically. Operators currently issuing paper bordereaux under OUG 31/2011 must migrate to the TRACE-DM-generated electronic format. Gap: this replaces an existing paper-based workflow with a mandatory digital one.

**E. Refusal Protocol**
Operators must implement a formal protocol for refusing waste intake when a citizen declines identity verification or declines to sign the household-origin declaration. This refusal must be operationally documented and staff must be trained on the enforcement basis (OUG 92/2021 Article 48 and Article 62).

**F. Enforcement Risk Management**
For repeated non-compliance, the National Environmental Guard may suspend an operator's right to use TRACE-DM for up to 30 days, which can trigger re-examination of the operator's environmental operating licence. Operators using road transport subcontractors for waste movement should include TRACE-DM compliance status as a vendor qualification criterion in subcontractor agreements.

**G. SIM Interoperability (AFM responsibility, workspace monitoring)**
The TRACE-DM platform will be interlinked with the Integrated Environmental System (SIM). While the technical integration is AFM's responsibility, operators should monitor AFM guidance for any data-field or reporting format requirements that flow from SIM interoperability.

*Source: Jurnalul.ro reporting on TRACE-DM draft HG, Jurnalul.ro, May 2026. https://jurnalul.ro/special-jurnalul/deseuri-municipale-reciclabile-sistem-electronic-trasabilitate-1032887.html | Legestart analysis, Legestart.ro, May 2026. https://legestart.ro/blog/instituirea-sistemului-trace-dm-si-reconfigurarea-trasabilitatii-deseurilor-municipale*
